name: page.tsx
summary: Next.js dynamic route page that renders a single feedback item's detail view, fetching the record via Prisma and displaying its analysis, topics, and status selector.

name: page.tsx
summary: Inbox listing page that parses search params for sentiment, status, topic, sort, and pagination filters, queries Prisma for feedback items and analyses, and renders a paginated, filterable table.

name: Badges.tsx
summary: Presentational React components rendering sentiment, severity, status, and topic badges with conditional Tailwind class names via the cn helper.

name: Filters.tsx
summary: Client-side filter bar component that reads and updates URL search params for sentiment, severity, status, topic, sort, and pagination using Next.js router and searchParams hooks.

name: StatusSelect.tsx
summary: Client component providing a dropdown to change a feedback item's status, persisting the update via fetch to the PATCH endpoint and refreshing the router on success.

name: github.ts
summary: GitHub issues ingestion adapter that builds an Octokit client, paginates issues with rate-limit parsing and exponential backoff, and normalizes results into the internal feedback item shape.

name: llm.ts
summary: OpenAI analysis module that builds prompts from feedback items, calls chat completions, validates responses with a Zod schema, and supports batch analysis with retry/backoff.

name: types.ts
summary: Shared constant definitions for sentiment labels, topic taxonomy, and feedback statuses used across the inbox UI and ingestion pipeline.

name: utils.ts
summary: Shared utility helpers including className concatenation, date formatting, text truncation, sleep, and exponential backoff delay used across UI and pipeline modules.

name: seed.ts
summary: Database seeding script that creates a demo user and populates sample feedback items with analyses for local development and testing.

name: route.ts
summary: Next.js catch-all route that wires the shared NextAuth authOptions into GET and POST handlers for the authentication endpoints.

name: route.ts
summary: Signup API endpoint that validates credentials with a Zod schema, checks for existing users, hashes passwords, and creates new user records.

name: route.ts
summary: Dashboard analytics API endpoint that aggregates feedback analyses by sentiment, status, severity, and topic, plus a daily feedback volume time series.

name: route.ts
summary: Feedback item API endpoints supporting fetching a single feedback item by id and patching its analysis (status, sentiment, severity, topics).

name: route.ts
summary: Feedback list API endpoint with filtering by sentiment, status, and topic, plus sorting and pagination over feedback items and their analyses.

name: auth.ts
summary: Configures NextAuth authOptions with credentials provider, JWT session strategy, and an authorize callback that validates email/password against the Prisma user table.

name: prisma.ts
summary: Prisma client singleton that instantiates a single PrismaClient instance and reuses it in development to avoid exhausting database connections.

name: handler.ts
summary: AWS Lambda entry point that invokes the shared ingest pipeline and returns a JSON summary of the run, with error handling that surfaces failures to the Lambda runtime.

name: ingest.ts
summary: Command-line script that runs the ingest pipeline once and prints the result summary, exiting with a non-zero code on failure for CI integration.

name: route.ts
summary: Next.js App Router POST handler that authenticates the session via NextAuth and triggers the ingest pipeline, returning JSON status responses.

name: route.ts
summary: Next.js App Router webhook endpoint for Slack integration, exposing GET (status check) and POST (send a Slack message) handlers gated by session auth and a Slack-enabled feature flag.

name: ingest.ts
summary: Core ingest pipeline orchestrator that fetches raw feedback items from a source, deduplicates against existing records, batches them through LLM analysis, persists results in a Prisma transaction, and notifies Slack on high-severity findings.

name: slack.ts
summary: Slack notification helper module providing a feature-flag check, a generic message sender via the Slack incoming webhook, and a high-severity feedback notifier that formats topics and sentiment for alerting.

name: page.tsx
summary: Server-rendered dashboard page that aggregates feedback analysis metrics (sentiment, severity, topic distribution, trend) via Prisma and renders KPI cards, Recharts visualizations, and a high-severity feedback list.

name: SentimentDonutChart.tsx
summary: Recharts-based donut chart component visualizing the distribution of positive, neutral, and negative sentiment counts.

name: SentimentTrendChart.tsx
summary: Recharts line chart component displaying daily sentiment trend counts over time for the dashboard.

name: SeverityChart.tsx
summary: Recharts bar chart component visualizing feedback counts grouped by severity level (1-5) for the dashboard.

name: TopicDistributionChart.tsx
summary: Recharts bar chart component showing the distribution of feedback items across detected topics for the dashboard.

name: .env.example
summary: Template of all runtime environment variables for FeedbackFlow AI, including NextAuth secrets, the Postgres DATABASE_URL, OpenAI API key and model, GitHub Issues source repo and token, ingest tuning knobs, and optional Slack/SMTP digest settings.

name: README.md
summary: Comprehensive project documentation covering features, tech stack, architecture, local quick-start, data model, LLM output format, project structure, AWS deployment, security, and acceptance-criteria mapping for FeedbackFlow AI.

name: amplify.yml
summary: AWS Amplify build specification for the Next.js frontend, running npm ci, prisma generate, and next build, then publishing the .next directory as build artifacts with node_modules and .next/cache caching.

name: docker-compose.yml
summary: Local development Docker Compose file defining a single postgres:16-alpine service (feedbackflow-db) on port 5432 with a persistent volume and a pg_isready healthcheck, used by the npm run db:up script.

name: package.json
summary: Root package manifest for the feedbackflow-ai Next.js 14 app, declaring scripts for dev/build/lint/typecheck, Prisma workflows, local db compose control, seed and ingest runners, Lambda bundling, and SAM validate/build/deploy, plus all runtime and dev dependencies.

name: tsconfig.json
summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the project targeting ES2022 with strict mode, bundler module resolution, JSX preserve, incremental builds, the Next.js plugin, and an @/* path alias mapping to ./src/*.

name: .understandignore
summary: Ignore-pattern file listing paths excluded from the understand-anything codebase analysis pipeline.

name: README.md
summary: AWS deployment guide covering backend provisioning (RDS, Lambda, EventBridge), schema migration, frontend Amplify deployment, and cost notes.

name: template.yaml
summary: AWS SAM/CloudFormation template provisioning VPC networking, an RDS Postgres instance, Secrets Manager credentials, and an EventBridge-scheduled Lambda ingest function.

name: next.config.mjs
summary: Next.js configuration enabling React strict mode and standalone output builds for containerized deployment.

name: postcss.config.js
summary: PostCSS configuration registering Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer plugins for the project's styling pipeline.

name: migration.sql
summary: Initial Prisma migration creating the core PostgreSQL tables (users, feedback_items, feedback_analyses, ingest_logs) with unique constraints and query indexes.

name: users
summary: Auth users table storing email, hashed password, name, and audit timestamps with a unique email constraint.

name: feedback_items
summary: Ingested raw feedback table keyed by source and external id for idempotent ingestion, with indexes on source and original timestamp.

name: feedback_analyses
summary: LLM analysis table linked 1:1 to feedback items via foreign key, storing sentiment, topics, severity score, summary, and triage status.

name: ingest_logs
summary: Observability log table recording each cron ingest run's source, status, item counts, and error details.

name: migration_lock.toml
summary: Prisma migration lock file pinning the database provider to postgresql to prevent cross-provider migrations.

name: schema.prisma
summary: Prisma schema defining the PostgreSQL data model with User, FeedbackItem, FeedbackAnalysis, and IngestLog models plus enums for triage and run statuses.

name: build-lambda.ts
summary: Build script that compiles the Lambda handler and bundles the Prisma client into the AWS Lambda deployment package.

name: layout.tsx
summary: Authenticated app route-group layout that gates access via a server-side session check and renders the Navbar around nested pages.

name: globals.css
summary: Global stylesheet with Tailwind CSS directives and base layer customizations for the application theme.

name: layout.tsx
summary: Root Next.js App Router layout that sets HTML metadata and wraps the application in the session Providers component.

name: page.tsx
summary: Login page rendering a credentials sign-in form with email/password fields, loading and error states, and post-login redirect handling.

name: page.tsx
summary: Root home page that immediately redirects visitors to the authenticated dashboard route.

name: page.tsx
summary: Signup page rendering a registration form that posts to the signup API, signs the new user in, and redirects to the dashboard.

name: Navbar.tsx
summary: Navigation bar component showing active-route links and a sign-out button, adapting to the current session state.

name: Providers.tsx
summary: Client-side wrapper component that mounts the NextAuth SessionProvider around the React tree.

name: middleware.ts
summary: NextAuth middleware that protects authenticated routes by validating the session token on each request.

name: next-auth.d.ts
summary: TypeScript module augmentation extending the NextAuth session and JWT types with a custom userId field.

name: tailwind.config.ts
summary: Tailwind CSS configuration defining the content scan paths, theme extensions, and the project's design tokens.

name: tsconfig.tsbuildinfo
summary: Auto-generated TypeScript incremental build cache storing compiler state for faster rebuilds.
